Title: Villager injured while driving away elephant herd in Sungai Siput
Article:
SUNGAI SIPUT: A man was injured after being attacked by a wild elephant while attempting to drive a herd away from his village in Kampung Orang Asli Perjek, RPS Legap, yesterday.
The Wildlife and National Parks Department (Perhilitan) said the incident occurred at about 9pm when a herd of four elephants entered the area behind villagers' homes and began feeding on cassava and banana crops.
Perhilitan said it received information on the incident at about 10pm before personnel from its Sungai Siput district office were sent to the village to conduct preliminary investigations.
"Interviews with villagers confirmed that the victim and several residents had attempted to chase the elephants away.
"During the attempt, the victim was standing too close to the herd before he was suddenly attacked by one of the elephants," the department said in a statement today.
The statement said the victim suffered injuries, bleeding and lost consciousness during the attack.
"Villagers managed to rescue him and bring him to safety before he was taken by ambulance to Sungai Siput Hospital at 10.30pm.
"He was later referred to Raja Permaisuri Bainun Hospital in Ipoh for further treatment and is reported to be in stable condition," it said.
Perhilitan said drone monitoring showed that the elephant herd had since left the village area.
"The department will continue monitoring the herd's movements and take appropriate management measures to reduce the risk of human-wildlife conflict in the area," it said.
The department will also assist the victim's family in applying for aid under the Wildlife Attack Victim Assistance initiative provided by the Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Ministry.
Perhilitan advised the public not to take matters into their own hands when encountering wildlife, particularly elephants, as doing so could endanger their safety.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Motorcyclist found dead hours after crash in Alor Gajah
Article:
ALOR GAJAH: A motorcyclist was found dead by the roadside about two hours after he is believed to have crashed along Jalan Air Tanai in Air Paabas here early today.
The victim, identified as 38-year-old Abd Rahim Yusuf, is believed to have been involved in the single-vehicle accident at about 5am.
His body was discovered by the public around 7am.
District police chief Superintendent Ahmad Abu Bakar said preliminary investigations found that the victim was riding a Modenas Kriss motorcycle from Kampung Air Tanai towards Jalan Air Paabas.
"Upon reaching the scene, the victim is believed to have lost control of the motorcycle, causing it to veer onto the left shoulder of the road.
"He sustained severe head injuries and was pronounced dead at the scene. His body was sent to the Forensic Unit of Alor Gajah Hospital for a post-mortem examination," he said when contacted today.
He said the victim, who was single and lived in Kampung Air Tanai, is believed to have left home to meet a friend before the accident.
The case is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987.
Those with information on the incident can contact investigating officer Inspector Loh Gia Xao at 019-538 2353 or visit the nearest police station to assist in the investigation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ukrainians protest for second day, call to replace army chief
Article:
KYIV: Protesters in Kyiv called for the dismissal of Ukraine's commander-in-chief, Oleksandr Syrskyi, after President Volodymyr Zelenskiy's removal of a popular defence minister sparked outrage and demands for a more modern approach to warfare.
Several thousand people gathered outside the presidential office for a second day after Mykhailo Fedorov, 35, a tech wizard who has been partly credited with Ukraine's recent successes in the war with Russia, was not reappointed in a surprise government reshuffle.
A 27-year-old protester named Andriy, a combat medic who lost his lower limb, came with a cardboard sign saying: "Cannot stand. Will not stay silent."
"Syrskyi had conflicts not only with the defence minister, but also with brigade and corps commanders. If he stays, we will simply be ineffective," said Andriy, whose brother died on the battlefield. "We could simply lose this war, we could lose," he added.
Zeliskiy provided little explanation for the reshuffle and said he had to step in since the rift between Syrskyi and Fedorov was so deep they won't sit at one table.
On Friday, he tapped Ukraine's former top cop, Ihor Klymenko, to lead the national security and defence council, replacing current chairman Rustem Umerov. Klymenko, who had initially been among the names as a likely Fedorov replacement, would be tasked with coordinating "all components of the security and defence sector", including defence production, the president added.
It was unclear whether Umerov, also Ukraine's top negotiator in US-backed peace talks with Russia, would be offered a new post. A top security operative overseeing Ukraine's long-range strikes against Russia, Yevhenii Khmara, was appointed as acting defence minister as Zelenskiy moved to calm down public outrage late on Thursday.
AT WHAT COST?
Protesters rallied for a second day outside Zelenskiy's office, chanting "Syrskyi away" and "Bring Fedorov back." Many called to listen to the voices of those currently fighting.
Fedorov's conflict with Syrskyi came into the open on Thursday after the former defence minister accused the 60-year-old general of sabotaging his work. Syrskyi, 60, played a key role in Kyiv's defence in the early days of the war and has been in his top post since early 2024. But he has faced harsh criticism for a rigid command style which some service members have said results in high troop losses and also for unwillingness to modernise combat.
Another protester named Nina, whose husband is serving, came to the demonstration for a second day, with one-year-old Oleksandr-Carlos. Her first demand is Syrskyi's dismissal.
"The problem isn't so much that Fedorov resigned as it is that Syrskyi remains in office," she said. The system under the commander-in-chief is more about showcasing results than prioritising people's lives, she said. "Sure, he deserves some credit, I don't deny that but at what cost? That's the question. Syrskyi is, after all, the old system," she said.
The protests resembled popular rallies last year at the same location over Zelenskiy's attempts to roll back the powers of Ukraine's anti-corruption agencies. He ultimately backtracked on those measures.
MODERN ARMY
On Friday, Zelenskiy held online consultations with military commanders. He also met Brigadier-General Andriy Biletsky, who commands Ukraine's Third Army Corps, to discuss the battlefield situation and defence plans.
In an attempt to show people that Ukraine would not backtrack from reforms introduced by Fedorov, he appointed Serhii Beskrestnov, a former adviser to the defence ministry, presidential adviser on defence technology.
Kyiv's latest political crisis erupted as Ukrainian forces have begun clawing back the initiative from Russia on the battlefield through long-range strikes on industry and logistics. Ukraine still faces deep problems in troop recruitment as Russian forces grind forward, as well as in securing enough air defences to repel deadly missile attacks on its cities.
Many Ukrainians believe that victory could only be achieved with technological advances and an army system that drops the worst practices from the Soviet Union times — a task, they say, impossible to complete under Syrskyi.
Protester Leonid, 63, a veteran, called himself a product of the Soviet army system, and agreed: "They are destroying our army. Maybe at some point he was necessary, but that is it." — REUTERS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kindergarten linked to neglect allegations told to halt operations pending probe
Article:
KUCHING: The Welfare Department has advised a Langkawi kindergarten implicated in allegations of child neglect and inappropriate treatment to halt operations temporarily until police complete their investigations.
Women, Family and Community Development Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri said that while the kindergarten is regulated by the Education Ministry, Welfare Deparment remains involved in child protection matters and is collaborating with relevant authorities in the investigation.
"The case is being investigated under Section 31(1) of the Child Act 2001. We have advised the kindergarten to temporarily cease operations until the investigation has been finalised.
"We also advise parents to follow the police's instruction to ensure the safety of their children," she told reporters after visiting the Pending Jetty upgrade project here today.
She said the Welfare Department, through its child protection officers, was collaborating with the police and the District Education Office to collect relevant information and prepare the reports required for the ongoing investigation.
– MORE
NANCY-KINDERGARTEN 2 (LAST) KUCHING
Nancy said the kindergarten operator had been cautioned over matters involving the care, safety and welfare of children, and reminded to adhere to the required regulations, stressing that appropriate action would be taken if any legal violations were detected.
Yesterday, Langkawi police chief assistant commissioner Khairul Azhar Nuruddin said investigations were ongoing into a report filed by a former
kindergarten
teacher over alleged improper handling of children at the premises, including claims of children being made to sleep on the floor without mattresses and being confined in a toilet.
He said investigations were being carried out to ascertain the validity of the claims, including through the examination of CCTV footage and witness testimony.
On the Pending Jetty upgrading project, Nancy, who is also the Santubong MP, said the RM600,000 project is now nearly 90 per cent complete and is expected to be completed on schedule on Aug 24.
She said the project would connect Pending Jetty with her parliamentary constituency area in Kampung Muhibbah Seberang, with the remaining works involving the construction of two sections of the jetty and stairs measuring a total length of about 12m to 13m.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Dropped as Johor exco, Aznan backs successor
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Former Johor education and information committee chairman Aznan Tamin has expressed confidence in Dr Muhammad Naqib Md Ghazali, who replaced him in the state exco line-up.
The Tanjung Surat assemblyman said every responsibility came with a timeframe, and that his struggle for the people had never hinged on a position.
"I will continue to go down to the ground, listen to the people and serve, regardless of position," he said in a Facebook post, in which he also congratulated Naqib.
"I am confident Naqib will ensure continuity and bring greater reforms," he said.
Aznan said the trust placed in the new exco was a great responsibility, as its members would have to ensure Johor continued to progress, bring prosperity to the people and realise the Maju Johor 2030 agenda.
During his time in office from 2024 to 2026, Aznan spearheaded several high-impact initiatives, including Sekolah Rintis Bangsa Johor, the Johor Talent Development Council and Insentif Mahasiswa Anak Johor.
Other programmes implemented under his leadership included Bantuan Laptop Siswa Johor, the transformation of Kolej Universiti Yayasan Pelajaran Johor, and free food aid and stationery kits for Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia candidates.
Earlier today, all
10 Johor executive councillors
took their oaths of office before the Regent of Johor, Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim, at Istana Bukit Serene here.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Form One student suffers fractured ankle in bullying case, three detained
Article:
KANGAR: Three Form One students have been detained to assist investigations into the alleged bullying of a schoolmate that left him with a fractured left ankle.
District police chief assistant commissioner Yusharifuddin Mohd Yusop said police received a report on the incident at 11.27pm on Thursday.
He said the victim's 39-year-old mother had earlier been informed by his class teacher at 12.28pm that her son, also a Form One student, had slipped while entering the classroom and was unable to walk.
The mother picked up her son at about 1pm and took him to Tuanku Fauziah Hospital here, where a doctor confirmed that he had fractured his left ankle.
Yusharifuddin said that while at the hospital at about 7.28pm, the boy told his mother that he had allegedly been bullied for several months, culminating in an incident that day which caused the injury.
The three students are believed to have kicked and hit the victim, thrown two large rubbish bins at his body and head, and spat on him. They are also suspected of extorting the victim by demanding his pocket money.
The three students have been remanded for two days from today to assist in the investigation.
The case is being investigated under Sections 324 and 507B of the Penal Code.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Muhyiddin says Bersatu remains sole opposition voice
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin has positioned his party as the country's only opposition force, claiming Perikatan Nasional had indirectly become pro-government through its political cooperation with Barisan Nasional.
His remarks came after
Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang said PN would assist BN
during the Negri Sembilan election campaign.
Muhyiddin said Pakatan Harapan and BN were part of the government at the federal and state levels, while PN could no longer claim complete independence after working with BN.
"Bersatu today finds itself in opposition, just as it did when the party was first established. In fact, the situation is even more challenging as Bersatu now stands as the sole opposition party in the Malaysian Parliament," he said in a Facebook post today.
"Why do I say Bersatu is the only one? It is because PH is the government at the federal level and in several states.
"BN is also the government at the federal level and in several states. When PN establishes political cooperation with BN, it also indirectly becomes a party that supports the government," he said.
Muhyiddin said this had created a major void in Malaysian politics, with no other credible party capable of providing effective checks and balances against government policies.
He said Bersatu began as an opposition party, with its establishment rooted in the struggle against kleptocracy linked to the 1Malaysia Development Bhd scandal.
He said the party must not forget those foundations now that it had returned to the opposition, adding that the circumstances it faced today were even more challenging.
Muhyiddin also accused PH and BN of sharing collective responsibility for policies that allegedly burdened the people, including higher diesel and petrol prices, the failure to effectively address living costs and delays in implementing institutional reforms.
"Barisan Nasional can loudly proclaim that it is time to bury Pakatan Harapan, and be greeted by smiles from Perikatan Nasional-Pas, but Barisan Nasional and Pakatan Harapan cannot escape even an inch from their collective responsibility," he said.
He accused Umno and BN of attempting to deflect blame while continuing to enjoy the benefits of being in government, before turning to calls for Malay and Muslim unity to secure support.
"Believe me, this is merely a charade to deceive the Malays and the people for the sake of gaining power," he said.
Muhyiddin added that Bersatu was free from such political manoeuvring and would remain committed to defending the people and advancing the country despite the challenges it faced.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MyCSP: Courier safety training programme opens for registration
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Postal Forum, a self-regulatory body designated by the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), today soft-launched registration for the Malaysia Courier Safety Passport (MyCSP), a structured safety training programme developed specifically for the courier industry.
In a statement today, Postal Forum said the MyCSP comprises six core modules, namely, Occupational Safety and Health; hazard and risk management in courier operations, basic ergonomics, road safety awareness, high-risk driving behaviours, and risk management and prevention.
The soft launch, held during the Courier Industry Safe Driving Challenge 2026 at MAEPS Serdang, has drawn an encouraging response from industry players, the statement added.
"Together, these modules provide participants with practical knowledge to improve workplace and road safety while supporting compliance with industry safety standards.
"With registration now open, courier service providers and personnel are encouraged to sign up ahead of the official launch later this year, with training set to begin in September 2026," the statement read.
Beyond strengthening courier personnel competency, the MyCSP is expected to establish a common safety benchmark for the industry, reduce accident-related costs, enhance professionalism among service providers, reinforce public confidence and support the sustainable growth of Malaysia's postal and courier sector.
Association of Malaysian Express Carriers (AMEC) president Ronald Tan described the MyCSP as a timely initiative that reflects the industry's shared commitment to strengthening safety standards.
"I encourage all courier service providers to embrace this programme and equip their personnel with the knowledge, skills and safety mindset needed to uphold the highest standards of workplace and operational safety," Tan said in the statement, extending his congratulations on the soft launch of MyCSP registration.
Meanwhile, Postal Forum head Nurhafizah Hanifah said the soft launch of MyCSP represents a key milestone in the industry's ongoing journey towards higher safety standards and greater professionalism.
"With registration now open, we are helping to ensure that courier personnel are better equipped to perform their duties responsibly and safely.
"Ultimately, this contributes to greater consumer confidence, as safer operations and higher service standards go hand in hand in delivering a more trusted and resilient courier ecosystem," she said.
The registration launch coincided with the finale of the Courier Industry Safe Driving Challenge 2026, jointly organised by the Association of Malaysian Express Carriers (AMEC) and Postal Forum.
The challenge saw 26 courier professionals from City-Link Express (M) Sdn Bhd, Federal Express Services (M) Sdn Bhd, GD Express Sdn Bhd, J&T Express (M) Sdn Bhd, MTR Services Sdn Bhd, Pos Malaysia and Skynet Worldwide (M) Sdn Bhd competing across the motorcycle, van and truck categories.
They were put through their paces with assessments testing their knowledge, practical skills, and operational readiness, all based on real-world driving and workplace scenarios.
Prior to the finale, participants went through a preliminary round on July 11, which tested their theoretical knowledge, vehicle inspection skills, and hazard perception.
The Safe Driving Challenge was also designed to boost safety awareness, drive the adoption of best practices, strengthen technical know-how in road and workplace safety, improve industry compliance, build a stronger safety culture, and foster healthy competition, teamwork and professional excellence across the courier sector.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: New Johor exco line-up to deliver manifesto pledges, drive Maju Johor 2030 agenda
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The newly appointed Johor state executive council (exco) members are determined to fulfil all pledges made in the manifesto for the recent state election and advance the Maju Johor 2030 agenda, ensuring that development benefits continue to reach Bangsa Johor.
Investment, trade, consumer affairs and human resources committee chairman Lee Ting Han, who was reappointed to the portfolio, stressed that the next four years would be a critical phase in advancing the agenda, including the Johor Economic Transformation Plan (JETP).
He expressed optimism that the agenda could be realised, citing the state's encouraging economic growth over two consecutive years.
"In the next four years, we must ensure that Johor's (development) progresses at a faster pace.
"We will continue with what has already been implemented, while any shortcomings or areas requiring improvement will be addressed to drive the state's transformation towards greater progress," he told reporters when met at the official residence of the Johor Menteri Besar here today.
Housing and Local Government Committee chairman Datuk Mohd Jafni Md Shukor said efforts under the Johor Affordable Housing Scheme (RMMJ) would continue, with a target of completing 30,000 housing units by the end of this year.
He said focus would also be given to continuing the Rumah Kasih Johor and Rumah Transit Bangsa Johor initiatives, as well as fulfilling all manifesto pledges made during the recent Johor state election.
"To ensure that this initiative has an impact on the people, we, under the leadership of Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, have set Key Performance Indicators (KPIs), with progress to be reviewed every three months. InsyaAllah, we will fulfil all manifesto pledges in phases," said Jafni, who previously held the same portfolio.
Education and Information Committee chairman Dr Muhammad Naqib Md Ghazali, who was appointed as an exco member for the first time, is determined to improve the government's information delivery system and further enhance education initiatives in the state.
"This is a great trust and responsibility that I have been entrusted with. It is also a new field for me, as I previously served in the health sector. InsyaAllah, I will strive to carry out this duty to the best of my ability," he said.
Unity, Heritage and Culture Committee chairman P. Pannir Selvam said his focus would be on strengthening the unity agenda based on the Bangsa Johor concept, as well as revitalising heritage programmes for the younger generation.
"My focus is to strengthen the identity of Bangsa Johor and preserve our culture and heritage, ensuring they continue to be appreciated by schoolchildren and future generations," he said.
The Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) manifesto for the 16th Johor state election outlined
63 pledges
anchored on six key thrusts aimed at preserving stability and sustaining the state's development momentum.
The manifesto, themed "Maju Johor, Kestabilan Dikekalkan, Kemajuan Diteruskan", was formulated based on the state government's governing experience and achievements over the past four years.
The Johor exco line-up for the new term saw six assemblymen retaining their portfolios, while four new faces were appointed to the state government administration.
All 10 assemblymen were
sworn in
before the Regent of Johor, Tunku Mahkota Ismail, during the appointment letter presentation and oath-taking ceremony held at Istana Bukit Serene here today.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump threatens to increase Canada tariffs over wildfire smoke pollution
Article:
NEW YORK: US President Donald Trump threatened on Friday a tariff increase on Canada to cover the cost of smoke pollution from wildfires that have choked large parts of the United States.
Dense wildfire smoke billowing down from Canada and northern Minnesota has set off unhealthy air quality alerts across the US.
As of Saturday, there were 937 active fires in Canada, most of which were burning out of control, according to the Canadian Wildland Fire Information System.
"This is Willful Negligence, and becoming a yearly occurrence, costing the United States Billions of Dollars," Trump said on Truth Social, adding that the "cost of this pollution must of necessity be added to the TARIFFS Canada is currently paying."
Trump accused Canada of "not properly maintaining" their forests, failing to carry out "basic Forest Management and Debris Removal."
He said he would call Prime Minister Mark Carney "to find out what they are going to do about" the smoke.
Canada's emergency management minister Eleanor Olszewski said Canada and the United States were in "constant contact," pointing toward their "long history of working together to fight wildfires."
She added that Canada has invested US$12 billion in forests sustainability and fire prevention since 2020.
Detroit, in the US Midwest, remained the most polluted city in the world on Friday, according to tracker IQAir. Washington and Chicago weren't far behind, and officials warned against spending unnecessary time outside.
The pollution triggered concern over the World Cup final on Sunday in an open stadium in New Jersey.
In New Jersey and New York, the metro area was experiencing air that could be unhealthy for sensitive groups, an improvement after smog on Thursday made the Manhattan skyline barely visible.
But the National Weather Service (NWS) warned the smoke may thicken overnight into Saturday morning.
Tournament organizers are "monitoring closely," White House World Cup task force executive director Andrew Giuliani told a briefing.
Peter Mullinax, a meteorologist for NWS, told AFP that winds over the Great Lakes could push more smoke into the Northeast, which could keep skies hazy.
He said forecasts for that region do expect some improvement.
"I don't believe that this should be as impactful as if you might be playing a game today," Mullinax said.
The issue for Sunday's game, said Joel Dreessen, an air quality forecaster for the state of Maryland, is whether more smoke spills south after weekend storm systems.
"Some of the models are starting to indicate that we'll start to pull down some smoke," he told AFP.
TRAVELLING TOXICITY
In cities across the Midwest and Northeast, people wore masks outdoors to filter out the dangerous air. In New York, libraries and train stations were handing them out for free.
Chris Carlsten, who studies the health impacts of fire smoke at the University of British Columbia, told AFP that the fine pollution particles from wildfires are particularly impactful on the lungs, whereas vehicle pollution skews slightly towards heart impacts.
He said plumes can be filled with wood and vegetation debris but also paint, plastic or metal.
And as smoke plumes travel, they undergo "photochemical aging," a series of reactions that Carlsten said "seem to make, from everything we understand in the chemistry, the aerosol more toxic."
The upper Midwest that is closer to the fires has faced especially bad air, with parts of Michigan, Minnesota and Wisconsin all recording air quality readings deep into the "hazardous" range for days.
CLIMATE CHANGE LINK
Advocates have stressed the connection between repeated episodes of wildfire smoke and climate change.
Mark Parrington, a scientist at the Copernicus Atmosphere Monitoring Service, told AFP that climate change was providing conditions for a longer fire season, with higher surface air temperatures and lower soil moisture.
So, he explained, "when there's an ignition we see these really large-scale, persistent burning where these fires can burn for weeks and weeks at a time through summer."
The blazes were worsening on Friday in Canada, especially in Ontario, according to authorities there.
Fires in Ontario have not caused any casualties, and several remote communities have been evacuated, as dozens of aircraft battle the blazes.
Meanwhile 16 active fires were burning in the Superior National Forest, on Minnesota's border with Canada.
"The forecast for erratic weather, shifting winds and the potential for isolated damaging wind gusts and thunderstorms will be a challenge for firefighting efforts," the US forest service said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: N9 polls: PH candidates in Jempol pledge to address infrastructure, Felda welfare issues
Article:
JEMPOL: Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidates contesting the Negri Sembilan state election in the four state seats under the Jempol parliamentary constituency have pledged to focus on resolving structural issues, improving basic amenities and safeguarding the welfare of Felda settlers.
For PH's Jeram Padang state seat candidate, G. Manivannan, his main focus is on addressing issues related to employment opportunities, education and infrastructure, which he described as the community's basic needs.
Drawing on nearly 20 years of political experience, including as former Kapar MP and political secretary to the PKR president said he is confident that voters in the traditionally Barisan Nasional (BN)-held seat are now more discerning in assessing the calibre of candidates.
"I believe the people want leaders who understand the structure of both the state and federal governments and are capable of bringing opportunities from both levels down to the grassroots.
"I came to Jeram Padang because I know there are problems here, and I am confident I can help solve them," he told reporters after the nomination process for the Serting, Palong, Jeram Padang and Bahau state seats at the Jempol District and Land Office here today.
Manivannan, a lawyer, will face a four-cornered contest against incumbent Datuk Mohd Zaidy Abdul Kadir of Barisan Nasional (BN), R. Sri Sanjeevan of Bersatu and Dayana Dal of Parti Orang Asli Malaysia (Asli).
Meanwhile, PH's Serting state seat candidate, Yaacob Mahmood, who has lived in Bandar Baru Serting for 43 years, is committed to championing the welfare of Felda settlers, particularly the second generation.
He said the issue of restrictions on electricity and water connections for second-generation settlers' homes is beginning to see a resolution after receiving serious attention from PH through Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
"Recently, the prime minister approved the request to allow electricity and water supply to be connected to the homes of second-generation settlers.
"This is a blessing and fulfils a long-standing request by the settlers' children," he said.
In Serting, Yaacob will face a three-cornered contest against incumbent Mohd Fairuz Mohd Isa of Perikatan Nasional (PN) and Bersatu candidate Muhammad Noraffendy Mohd Salleh @ Affendy Salleh.
PH candidate for the Palong state seat, Mohd Zahin Zinal Abidin, described his candidacy as a major responsibility.
A second-generation Felda settler, he said issues affecting the future of the Felda community would be at the heart of his campaign in the state election.
"Our main focus is to examine and champion critical issues affecting the second generation, particularly in the areas of housing, welfare and economic empowerment," said Zahin, who lives in Felda Palong 8 here.
In the upcoming state election, Mohd Zahin will face incumbent Datuk Mustapha Nagoor of BN and Bersatu candidate Rebin Birham in a three-cornered contest.
Meanwhile, the Bahau state seat will see a straight fight between incumbent and Negeri Sembilan DAP vice-chairman Teo Kok Seong and BN candidate Chong Fui Ming.
The
Election Commission
(EC) has set July 28 for early voting and Aug 1 as polling day for the Negri Sembilan state election.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] RM200mil KWAP losses: MACC opens probe into eFishery investment
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has launched an investigation into the reported RM200 million investment losses suffered by the Retirement Fund (Incorporated) (KWAP), involving Indonesian aquaculture technology company eFishery.
MACC chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman said an investigation team had been formed to conduct a comprehensive review and examination of the matter.
"The investigation will be conducted fairly, transparently and impartially in accordance with existing legal provisions.
"The public are urged not to speculate on
the investigation
to avoid confusion and to safeguard the integrity of the probe process," he said in a statement.
Earlier today, KWAP said it is continuing to pursue all available avenues to maximise the recovery of its investment in eFishery.
This follows the Finance Ministry's written reply last Thursday, which said KWAP had been deceived over its investment in eFishery and that it was a well-planned fraud.
The ministry said the Indonesian aquaculture startup's management had manipulated its financial statements.
Following the discovery, the ministry said the consortium of investors, including KWAP, had taken firm measures, including legal action, fund recovery efforts, internal governance reviews and strengthened controls.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MCA Youth sec-gen told to stay away from Negri Sembilan campaign
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: MCA Youth secretary-general Saw Yee Fung said she had been told she could excuse herself from Barisan Nasional's (BN) campaign for the Negri Sembilan election after questioning the coalition's cooperation with Pas.
Saw said the instruction was sent via WhatsApp and shared a screenshot of the message from party secretary-general Datuk Chong Sin Woon on Facebook. It read: "Yes, you do not need to come for the Negri Sembilan state election. Thank you."
Saw had questioned whether the party leadership endorsed BN's cooperation with Pas ahead of the Negri Sembilan election.
She also said no party leader had responded to her concerns on the night BN announced its candidates for the Negri Sembilan election.
"Some argue that this was a decision by the party leadership, and therefore we must comply.
"However, to date, the Central Committee has never convened a meeting to discuss this matter, nor has any related information, direction, or instruction been shared in the WhatsApp group.
"No leader has told me, either publicly or privately, what direction or decision I should follow," she said in a Facebook post last night.
While campaigning for BN in the Johor election, Saw was reported as saying she repeatedly told voters that BN was not working with Pas.
She was also reported to have urged BN supporters to vote for the coalition to stop the "green wave", while expressing disappointment over BN's cooperation with Pas in Negri Sembilan.
"I understand that the President (Datuk Seri Dr Wee Ka Siong) was in London at the time and could not reply immediately due to the time difference.
"However, the MCA secretary-general, who is leading the campaign for the Negri Sembilan election, also did not offer any response.
"So, it is not that I failed to ask; it is that no one was willing to answer," she added.
Saw said MCA had switched seats with DAP by suppressing opinions within the party.
She said MCA was becoming what it once despised.
"We have always criticised the DAP for suppressing freedom of speech.
"Today, have we simply switched seats, changed our mindsets, and under the stewardship of certain leaders, ended up becoming the very thing we once despised?
"I love this party.
"But the party I love is the MCA that is willing to listen, willing to deliberate, and accommodating of diverse voices—not the version today that is slowly becoming unrecognisable," she said.
On July 14, media reported that Perikatan Nasional (PN) information chief Tan Sri Annuar Musa said PN and Umno had found common ground on cooperation for the Negri Sembilan election.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Founding father: Statues of Myanmar's Aung San disappear
Article:
YANGON, Myanmar: Statues of independence hero Aung San are quietly disappearing across Myanmar in a contest over his legacy as father of both the nation and of detained democracy figurehead Aung San Suu Kyi.
General Aung San fought with and against both the British and the Japanese colonisers as he sought independence, but was assassinated on July 19, 1947, just months before his dream was realised.
Considered the father of modern-day Myanmar, his statues have dotted the country for decades.
Many were added while Suu Kyi's party was in power – and many are now being taken down by the government of Min Aung Hlaing, the coup leader who ousted her in 2021.
In Yangon's Thu Mingalar Park, an AFP reporter this week noted the absence of one towering likeness installed almost a decade ago, grass newly planted in its place.
The government has acknowledged taking down some statues it said had "incorrect proportions and shapes."
But analysts say the removals may be an attempt to erase from public spaces a powerful symbol linked to Suu Kyi, the popular Nobel laureate who remains detained.
Aung San is "engraved in people's memory" as Myanmar's independence hero, and many see his legacy being continued by his daughter, according to Myanmar specialist Moe Thuzar.
- Historical legacy -
While in power from 2016-2020, during Myanmar's decade-long experiment with democracy, Suu Kyi's National League for Democracy (NLD) put up statues and also returned Aung San's face to local currency notes.
They could be seen as "efforts to revive his historical legacy", Moe Thuzar said.
Since the coup, which triggered a devastating civil war, the military has focused on making Suu Kyi and the NLD "politically irrelevant", she added.
But erasing the memory of Suu Kyi's father is unlikely.
The murder of Aung San and several of his colleagues by local rivals is marked each year as Martyrs' Day, a public holiday when thousands pay their respects at the Yangon mausoleum that holds the victims' remains.
"They can remove the statues, but they can never erase Bogyoke's image," one Yangon resident said, using the Burmese word for general to describe Aung San.
"They already hold power, and they have the weapons and the military. I don't know what they are still so afraid of," the 34-year-old said, speaking anonymously due to security concerns.
'INCORRECT PROPORTIONS'
After local media reported the removal of several statues, government spokeswoman Khaing Khaing Soe said earlier this month that only those with "incorrect proportions and shapes are being systematically reviewed and addressed."
Since 2016, she said in a statement, a review of Aung San statues in more than 100 townships had found some had features "inconsistent with the dignity of such an important historical figure."
"Systematic maintenance efforts are being carried out" to prevent "any disrespect toward the commemorative statues", and to ensure "future generations can continue to study Myanmar's historical heritage in a correct and appropriate manner", she added.
One statue installed during the NLD administration was removed earlier this month from Mya Kan Thar Park in Yangon, a local resident said.
Another resident in the township said the statue had been "totally fine, with good proportions."
In late June, authorities also removed a statue from a park in Mudon, in southern Mon state, said a resident speaking anonymously.
Authorities later covered the foundation of the 2019 statue with a plastic sheet, the resident said, adding: "No one has dared to complain."
NOT EVERYONE'S HERO
Despite his national hero status, Aung San is feted mostly by ethnic majority Bamar elites, rather than the ethnic minority groups who have fought against central rule for decades, researcher Morgan Michaels said.
Under the Suu Kyi-led government, there were several arguments with ethnic minority groups over the NLD's attempts to erect statues or name infrastructure after Aung San, said Michaels, a Myanmar specialist with the International Institute for Strategic Studies.
The Kachin and the Karenni, for example, "have their own heroes."
"They don't appreciate the imposition... of his likeness and the symbolism that is attached to him, because it's also a symbol of the Bamar dominance of the state," he said.
Since the coup, some of Myanmar's myriad ethnic minority factions have banded with pro-democracy guerrillas to fight the armed forces.
A politician from one ethnic minority group, who declined to be named, acknowledged Aung San's struggle for independence but accused the NLD of erecting statues "for their party's interest."
"It's not necessary to keep many of his statues," the politician said, adding of the NLD: "Their era is finished now."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: N9 polls: Youngest candidate in five-cornered Sri Tanjung fight
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The 16th Negri Sembilan state election has spotlighted M. Leevineshwaraan, the youngest candidate contesting for a seat and seeking the people's mandate.
The 23-year-old, representing Bersatu, will face a five-way battle for the Sri Tanjung state seat as he seeks to prove himself to voters.
Leevineshwaraan's biggest challenge comes from incumbent Datuk Dr G. Rajasekaran of Pakatan Harapan (PH), who secured the seat in the previous election with a 3,996-vote majority.
In the 15th state polls in 2023, Muhammad Syakir Fitri Sadri held the record as the youngest candidate, contesting the Paroi state seat as an Independent at the age of 25.
Sri Tanjung is one of five state seats within the Port Dickson constituency, where 19,590 registered voters are eligible to vote in the upcoming election.
At the other end of the age spectrum, Barisan Nasional (BN) deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan and PH's Abd Latif A Tambi, both aged 70, are the senior-most candidates in this year's state election.
Mohamad, who is defending the Rantau seat, and Abd Latif, contesting in Gemencheh, will each go head-to-head in a two-way race.
In the previous state election, the oldest candidate was Bujang Abu, 72, who stood as an Independent.
However, Bujang will not be contesting in this election after failing to provide the documents required by the Election Commission (EC).
The Rantau state seat, within the Rembau parliamentary constituency, has 34,831 eligible voters, while Gemencheh, within the Tampin parliamentary constituency, has 24,916 eligible voters.
Nine of the 103 candidates contesting the state polls are women, with PH fielding the highest number, at four.
The number marks a slight increase over the previous election, in which eight of the 83 candidates were women.
The EC has announced that early voting will take place on July 28, with polling day scheduled for Aug 1.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Motorcyclist remains in ICU after fatal East Coast Highway RXZ convoy crash
Article:
KUANTAN: A motorcyclist critically injured in the Yamaha RXZ convoy crash that claimed four lives on the East Coast Expressway on July 11 remains in the intensive care unit (ICU).
Kuantan police chief Assistant Commissioner Ashari Abu Samah said five victims remained in hospital, with three being treated at Tengku Ampuan Afzan Hospital (HTAA), including one in the ICU. Another injured victim is receiving treatment at a private hospital in Kuantan, while one is recuperating at Sultanah Nur Zahirah Hospital in Kuala Terengganu.
"So far, only five remain in hospital and eight injured riders have been discharged from hospital," he told the New Straits Times.
The 1.20am crash near the Jabor toll plaza exit involved more than 30 motorcycles allegedly racing, killing four riders and injuring at least 20 others.
One of the motorcyclists rammed into the rear of a Toyota Vios, triggering a chain-reaction crash as other riders failed to avoid the collision and crashed into one another. Three riders died at the scene, while the fourth succumbed to injuries at HTAA.
Meanwhile, Ashari said investigations were ongoing and police had so far recorded statements from only five of the motorcyclists involved in the crash.
"We have yet to record statements from the other riders who were part of the convoy. Police have obtained statements from the Vios driver, his passenger, and three other motorists who were about 200m from the Vios at the time of the crash," he said.
Ashari said the case was being investigated for causing death by reckless or dangerous driving.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BN to launch Negri Sembilan election manifesto on July 24
Article:
JELEBU: Negri Sembilan Barisan Nasional (BN) will launch its manifesto for the state election on July 24.
Negri Sembilan Umno liaison committee chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias said the manifesto would focus on various aspects, including the state development agenda, education, the people's well-being and administrative stability.
He said the manifesto would serve as a continuation of the approach implemented during the previous state administration, while also introducing several improvements based on the current needs of the people.
"BN's manifesto will certainly be based on the way forward.
"We are looking at improvements and added value based on what we have experienced under the unity government over the past two years and eight months. Overall, this is reflected in BN's manifesto.
"This time, improvements in several segments, particularly development, education, welfare and the stability of the people, will be given attention," he said.
He was speaking after the nomination process at Dewan Besar Kuala Klawang here today.
Jalaluddin said the manifesto would also seek to address the rising cost of living, especially among low-income groups, while providing greater support for small traders.
"This is so they can generate suitable income to improve their circumstances and also contribute towards enhancing the state's economy.
"Because, in my view, the comfort and well-being of people at the grassroots level are the biggest contributors to the state's stability and prosperity," he said.
Asked about the BN candidate for menteri besar if the coalition receives the mandate, Jalaluddin said the matter would only be decided after the state election results are known.
He said the decision would rest with the BN top leadership.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BN candidate says Linggi stronghold cannot be taken for granted
Article:
PORT DICKSON: Despite Linggi being regarded as a Barisan Nasional (BN) stronghold, incumbent Datuk Mohd Faizal Ramli is not taking the challenge lightly in the Negri Sembilan election.
This is especially so with caretaker Negri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun entering the race for Pakatan Harapan (PH), alongside his former contender in the 2023 election, Datuk Zamri Md Said, who is contesting again.
"Although Linggi is described as a BN stronghold, it is not something that can be taken lightly.
"I will not consider this an advantage because every opportunity and every available space must be used in the best possible way," he told reporters after the nomination process today.
Faizal said he had been entrusted by the party to defend the Linggi seat and would work closely with the entire BN machinery to ensure the party retained the constituency.
He said the focus would be on maintaining the support of BN's core supporters while reaching out to voters during the campaign period.
He also called for a peaceful election campaign, urging all parties to avoid excessive provocation, slander and accusations.
He said Linggi's diverse village communities had long lived together harmoniously and that political competition should not create divisions among residents.
"We do not want any division after the election because we will meet again.
"Tomorrow, we will still live together as neighbours and members of the same community," he said.
In the last state election, Faizal won the seat with 7,832 votes, while Zamri received 6,371 votes, giving Faizal a majority of 1,461 votes.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BN-PN understanding in Negri polls not a merger, says Wee
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The strategic understanding between Barisan Nasional and Perikatan Nasional for the Negri Sembilan election does not amount to a merger, says MCA president Datuk Seri Dr Wee Ka Siong.
He said the arrangement was aimed at avoiding multi-cornered contests that could split votes and lead to political instability, while allowing the parties to work towards forming a stable and capable government in Negri Sembilan.
"An understanding does not mean a merger. Each party will continue to maintain its ideology, stance and role as a check and balance," he said in a statement.
Wee said MCA's participation in the Aug 1 polls was to ensure that the interests of the Chinese community, Malaysia's multiracial society, vernacular education and religious freedom continued to have representation and a voice in government.
He said the cooperation was based on the interests of the people and was not unconditional support, adding that MCA would oppose any policy affecting the rights and interests of Malaysia's diverse communities.
"The people need a government that can resolve issues such as water supply, roads, floods, employment, business affairs and the rising cost of living, rather than endless political disputes," he said.
Wee added that the Negri Sembilan election would provide voters with an opportunity to choose representatives who were capable, responsive to public concerns and able to fulfil their promises.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hadi dismisses Muhyiddin's 'toxic PN' claim, points finger at Bersatu
Article:
JEMPOL: Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang has rejected Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin's claim that Perikatan Nasional (PN), under the Islamist party's leadership, has become "toxic".
Hadi instead criticised Bersatu, blaming the party for the strained ties between the two parties in the opposition bloc.
"That is not true.
"What happened was Bersatu's misconduct in the cooperation," he told reporters after accompanying PN candidates during the nomination process for the Negri Sembilan election here today.
It was reported yesterday that Muhyiddin claimed PN had become "toxic" under Pas' leadership, with component parties increasingly being excluded from the decision-making process. (hyperlink: https://www.nst.com.my/news/nation/2026/07/1491090/muhyiddin-pn-toxic-under-pas-leadership-bersatu-eyes-new-coalition)
Muhyiddin also accused Pas of leaving Bersatu in the lurch after failing to continue seat negotiations ahead of the Negri Sembilan election.
Asked about Muhyiddin's statement that Bersatu planned to form a new political coalition with several parties after the Negri Sembilan election, Hadi said it was up to them.
Hadi said PN was not rushing into making any decisions, adding that the coalition wanted to focus on cooperation that brings "blessings and saves the people".
On the understanding reached between PN and Barisan Nasional (BN) for the state election, Hadi said the cooperation had received positive feedback from Pas grassroots.
He also said efforts would be made to expand the cooperation between the two coalitions beyond the Negri Sembilan election.
"We will work towards expanding such cooperation. What is important is that our country remains safe," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor Regent tells new exco to put people's interests first
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Regent of Johor, Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim, has urged the new Johor executive council (exco) to ensure every policy and decision benefits the state and is guided by the interests of the people.
In his decree today, he said exco members must go to the ground to identify and understand the needs of the community.
"His Royal Highness Tunku Mahkota Ismail, the Acting Sultan of Johor, has reminded all members of the Johor exco to always discharge their entrusted duties and responsibilities with the utmost dedication, integrity and sincerity.
"His Royal Highness emphasised that every policy and decision made must be centred by the interests of the people and benefit the state of Johor as a whole," the Johor royal press office said in a statement today.
Tunku Ismail also decreed that the delivery of the state government's services must be carried out with care.
"The Acting Sultan of Johor also decreed that all Johor exco members must regularly go to the ground to understand the needs of the community, while ensuring that the delivery of government services is executed efficiently, effectively and with integrity," the statement added.
Earlier today, the Regent graced the Appointment and Oath of Allegiance Ceremony for the Johor exco members at the Balai Mengadap, Istana Bukit Serene, in Johor Baru.
Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced the new exco line-up today after Barisan Nasional won 48 of the 56 seats in the July 11 state election.
Six exco members retained their portfolios while four new faces were appointed.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Police rescue kidnap-for-ransom victim, detain six suspects
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police rescued a kidnapped man within 48 hours and arrested six suspects following a RM7 million ransom demand.
Federal police Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar said the victim was rescued from a residential unit in i-City, Shah Alam, at 1am today.
"Police received a report at 1.30am yesterday from the victim's 46-year-old wife, who said her husband had been abducted and that the suspects had demanded RM7 million for his release," he said.
He said a team comprising Selangor Criminal Investigation Department and Special Branch personnel later raided the residential unit and rescued the victim.
The six people arrested comprised five men and a woman, aged between 30 and 46.
Police also seized various types of drugs, several mobile phones and vehicles.
Kumar said the case is being investigated under Section 3(1) of the Kidnapping Act for kidnapping for ransom, which carries a jail term of between 30 and 40 years and caning upon conviction.
"All six suspects have been remanded for 10 days under Section 12 of the Kidnapping Act. Investigations under the Dangerous Drugs Act are also being conducted against all those arrested," he said.
Kumar said investigations were ongoing to identify others involved, as well as the motive and criminal network linked to the case.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: When silence enables bullying
Article:
BULLYING has evolved. Today, it is less visible, more psychological and often more damaging.
Bullying is about power. It is rarely about physical strength alone. Crucially, not all bruises are visible.
The most painful form of bullying is often emotional and relational.
It includes  constant criticism that erodes self-worth, the silent treatment used as punishment,  public humiliation disguised as  jokes,  exclusion from meetings or social circles,  spreading  rumours and repeated intimidation that leaves a person anxious and fearful.
Victims begin to question their own value, abilities and even their sanity. This form of bullying exists everywhere. It happens in schools, universities, workplaces, homes and online.
The common denominator is not age or setting — it is the misuse of power. In Malaysia, we have witnessed students being mocked repeatedly until they dread going to school.
We hear of young professionals  resigning because of toxic workplace cultures where humiliation is normalised in the name of discipline or performance.
We know of domestic relationships where one partner constantly belittles, threatens, humiliates, controls finances, or isolates the other from family and friends.
Social media has also become a platform where cyberbullying, public shaming, and character assassination spread rapidly, often without accountability.
Victims often carry their emotional pain in silence.
They suppress fear, anger, shame, and helplessness because they worry they will not be believed or face  consequences for speaking out.
A teacher notices a child being isolated but assumes it is "children being children".
Colleagues watch a staff member being humiliated by a superior but avoid speaking up for fear of becoming the next target.
Family members know that emotional abuse is happening behind closed doors but dismiss it as "a private family matter".     Friends witness cyberbullying yet  scroll past.
Over time, these unexpressed emotions accumulate. The emotional discomfort becomes overwhelming.
Perhaps the greatest concern is not that bullying exists, but that we have become accustomed to it.
We witness it, we hear about it, and we recognise it — yet we remain silent.
Silence allows bullying to grow. Every ignored incident sends a message that such behaviour is acceptable.
Every bystander who chooses not to act unintentionally strengthens a culture of intimidation.
If we carry unresolved emotional wounds, struggle with negative emotions, or face repeated conflicts in our relationships,  we should have the courage to seek help.
Addressing the root causes of our emotional pain is not a sign of weakness; it is an act of responsibility.
Left unaddressed, these  issues can become  ingrained in our character, shaping the way we think, relate to others and respond to life's challenges.
Over time, they may impair our relationships, decision-making, and overall psychological well-being.
Healing is not only for our own benefit — it also prevents our pain from becoming someone else's burden.
Creating psychologically safe environments is therefore everyone's responsibility.
Parents must model respectful communication at home. Schools should teach empathy, emotional regulation, and conflict resolution alongside academic excellence.
Employers must cultivate workplaces where respect, dignity, and accountability are non-negotiable. Community leaders, religious institutions, and policymakers should continue promoting awareness while ensuring that support systems are accessible and trusted.
Ultimately, true strength is not measured by how much power we have over others, but by how well we manage ourselves.
I do not need another victim to become a hero.
Real heroes empower rather than intimidate. They protect rather than exploit. They choose compassion over control and courage over silence.
If we  want to build a healthier Malaysia, we must stop treating bullying as someone else's problem.
It begins with recognising the problem, refusing to normalise it, and having the courage to act before silence claims another victim.
ASSOCIATE PROFESSOR DR HANIZA RAIS
Malaysian Board of Counsellors
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Negri Sembilan polls: Chuah, Lukut and Bagan Pinang contests take shape
Article:
PORT DICKSON: The Chuah seat will see a straight fight in the Negri Sembilan election.
The Lukut and Bagan Pinang seats will have three-cornered contests, while Sri Tanjung will see a five-cornered contest.
The Election Commission (EC) confirmed today that Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate Datuk Yew Boon Lye will defend Chuah against Pau Jeou Ching of Barisan Nasional (BN) after nominations closed.
EC returning officer Nurhazelin Makli said the nomination papers submitted by both candidates had been accepted by the commission.
"I received two nomination papers. The first was from the PH candidate at 9.02am, followed by the BN candidate at 9.06am.
"I checked all the nomination papers and none of them was rejected," she said during a brief media conference at the Port Dickson District Administration Complex today.
Meanwhile, PH candidate for Lukut Choo Ken Hwa will defend the seat against Sathes Kumar Nilameham of Perikatan Nasional (PN) and Teo Seng Li, an independent candidate.
The Bagan Pinang contest will involve a three-cornered battle, with incumbent Abd Fatah Zakaria of PN taking on PH's Nasir Rahman and Sheikh Junaidy Jamaludin of Bersatu.
In Sri Tanjung, incumbent Datuk Dr G Rajasekaran of PH will face BN's A. Achutan, M. Leevineshwaraan of Bersatu, Datuk A. Saravanan, an independent candidate, and Islah Wahyudi Zainudin, also an independent candidate.
The EC has set Aug 1 as polling day, with early voting scheduled for July 28.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man, 66, arrested over rape of disabled teen in Tawau
Article:
TAWAU: A 66-year-old man has been arrested and remanded for seven days over the rape of a 15-year-old girl with disabilities.
Acting Tawau police chief Superintendent Wilynton Enchana Watt said the case came to light after the teenager told her adoptive parent that she was experiencing bodily pain.
The victim, who has attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD), was taken to Tawau Hospital for a medical examination.
A report was lodged, and the suspect was arrested at a house in Kampung Ranggu.
The suspect was brought to court this morning, where a remand order was granted.
Wilynton said the suspect is being investigated under Section 376(1) of the Penal Code for rape. The offence is punishable by up to 30 years' jail and whipping.
Police also seized several items believed to be connected to the case, including a collared T-shirt, a pair of jeans, a cap and other belongings.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Lipis route under police watch amid migrant smuggling attempts
Article:
KUANTAN: The Lipis stretch remains under close police surveillance as smuggling syndicates continue to use the route to ferry undocumented migrants from a neighbouring country into Malaysia.
Last year, police arrested 56 undocumented Myanmar nationals – 29 men and 27 women – along with eight Malaysians who acted as transporters for the migrants during a series of integrated operations in Lipis.
Pahang acting police chief Datuk Azry Akmar Ayob said the undocumented migrants were believed to have been smuggled through Kelantan before entering Pahang.
He said that, as of June 30 this year, Lipis police had arrested two transporters and 11 undocumented migrants attempting to travel to Kuala Lumpur, believed to be the final destination for many of them.
"There has been a significant movement of undocumented migrants using the Lipis route towards Kuala Lumpur. We are aware of these activities through integrated operations (Ops Bersepadu).
"We have identified several hotspots along the Lingkaran Tengah Utama (LTU) Expressway and the trunk road. Besides regular roadblocks and enforcement operations, our operations are intelligence-led and supported by public tip-offs," he told the New Straits Times.
Azry Akmar said smugglers were likely to continue changing their tactics, including exploiting toll-free routes such as the LTU Expressway connecting Lipis and Bentong.
"The Lipis stretch is not the main smuggling corridor but rather one of several routes exploited by criminal networks to transport undocumented migrants into the country.
"Authorities remain on high alert to curb human trafficking and migrant smuggling by strengthening security at the state's border in Lipis," he said.
He urged members of the public to provide information on suspicious activities or large groups of individuals believed to be involved in migrant smuggling.
On July 6, a BMW carrying five
undocumented Myanmar migrants crashed into a water tank along Jalan Lipis-Merapoh while fleeing a police roadblock. The 45-year-old local driver, who tested positive for drugs, and the migrants were arrested.
In 2024, police arrested nine undocum nted Myanmar nationals, including a 12-year-old girl, travelling in two separate vehicles during roadblocks. In 2022, police detained 14 men crammed inside a multi-purpose vehicle in Lipis.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 4 killed, 4 missing in Vietnam flash floods
Article:
HANOI: At least four people were killed and four others were missing after a flash flood swept through a mountainous village in Vietnam's northern province of Lai Chau, state media reported on Saturday.
The flood in Muong Than village early on Friday after days of heavy rains in several parts of northern Vietnam also injured seven people, reported the Vietnam News Agency.
Vietnam is prone to deadly storms and floods during the rainy season that peaks from July through September. Natural disasters, mostly floods, killed 489 people in the country last year, according to government data.
The Vietnam News Agency report had photos showing red mud and flood water blanketing the village, with rocks and wood logs piling in a corner of a damaged road.
Since Wednesday, landslides and flash floods triggered by heavy rain have also damaged roads, power grids and hundreds of houses, as well as inundating 238 hectares of crops in the region, according to the government's disaster management agency.
Several parts of northern Vietnam are forecast to receive heavy rain of up to 250mm on Saturday, the agency said, warning of more flash floods and landslides. -REUTERS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PH banking on Aminuddin's MB track record in Negri polls, says Fahmi
Article:
JEMPOL: Pakatan Harapan will anchor its Negri Sembilan election campaign on its governance record and the leadership of caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun.
PH communications director Datuk Seri Fahmi Fadzil said Aminuddin's administration had delivered tangible improvements in the economy, development and zakat collection.
"I believe the stability he has provided, with no scandals, no major issues and no problems throughout his eight years in office, is something we must continue.
"We also want to ensure continuity and maintain close cooperation between the state and federal governments. This will be one of our key campaign messages throughout the state election," he said.
Fahmi said this after nominations at the Jempol District Administrative Complex today.
Asked about cooperation between Barisan Nasional and Perikatan Nasional, Fahmi said it would not hinder PH's efforts to retain the state.
"Our election machinery is fully prepared to defend the PH-led state government," he said.
Fahmi also urged all parties to avoid slander and refrain from exploiting issues involving race, religion and royalty.
The nomination centre at the complex covers four state constituencies — Serting, Palong, Jeram Padang and Bahau.
In Serting, PH's Yaacob Mahmood will face Fairuz Isa of PN and Muhammad Noraffendy Mohd Salleh of Bersatu.
Palong will see a three-cornered contest involving PH's Zahin Zinal Abidin, Datuk Mustapha Nagoor of BN and Bersatu's Rebin Birham.
In Jeram Padang, PH's G. Manivannan is up against Datuk Zaidy Abdul Kadir of BN, Bersatu's R. Sri Sanjeevan and Dayana Dal of Parti Asli.
Bahau will witness a straight fight between Teo Kok Seong of PH and BN's Chong Fui Ming.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police warn parties against touching sensitive issues during Negri Sembilan campaign
Article:
PORT DICKSON: Parties contesting the Negri Sembilan election have been reminded to avoid raising sensitive issues, particularly those involving religion, royalty and race (3R), throughout the campaign period.
Negri Sembilan police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad said candidates and party supporters must refrain from making provocative, inflammatory or defamatory statements that could spark public tension, hatred or racial division.
"We urge all contesting parties, especially candidates, to focus their speeches on highlighting their respective parties' strengths without making statements that could cause public concern or anxiety," he told reporters at the Port Dickson District Administration Complex today.
Alzafny also warned that enforcement action would be taken against candidates or supporters who breached traffic and campaign regulations, including riding motorcycles without helmets during election-related activities.
He said all party representatives had been briefed on the dos and don'ts during the campaign period, adding that enforcement would be carried out in accordance with existing laws.
Meanwhile, Alzafny said the nomination process for the Negri Sembilan election proceeded smoothly, with all eight nomination centres completing the process without any major issues.
He said the police's main priority was to ensure the nomination process was conducted safely and without any untoward incidents.
"Based on feedback received, the situation at all eight nomination centres was under control.
"Party supporters also complied with the instructions and advice given by the police," he said.
He added that the situation at all nomination centres remained calm and orderly from early morning, with supporters dispersing immediately after returning officers announced the list of candidates.
A total of 2,373 police officers and personnel were deployed across all nomination centres to manage security, maintain public order and control traffic.
Alzafny said police observations showed that the number of supporters at each nomination centre ranged from 1,000 to 2,500.
Jempol recorded the highest turnout, with about 2,500 supporters, while Kuala Pilah recorded the lowest, with around 1,000.
He added that police had not received any reports of incidents related to the nomination process.
"We would like to thank everyone, especially party supporters, for maintaining order, harmony and complying with all regulations and instructions issued," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Too much, not too little: Deputy premier Dr Sim says overeating is now Sarawak’s bigger health challenge
Article:
KUCHING, July 18 — Public health policies should place greater emphasis on tackling overnutrition, as it is a major contributor to the rising incidence of diabetes among Sarawakians, said Deputy Premier Datuk Amar Dr Sim Kui Hian.
Dr Sim said while undernutrition remains an issue, it affects only a small percentage of the population compared with overnutrition, which now requires greater attention.
“Undernutrition is still there, not to say that we don’t have it, maybe two or three per cent, but overnutrition is 50 per cent. Isn’t that also a problem?
“In the past, we focused too much on that two or three per cent, but now we also need to focus on the 50 per cent.
“Because this 50 per cent are at risk of diabetes and could suffer many health complications, including heart attacks, requiring dialysis, amputations and even blindness,” he told reporters after officiating at a free fundoscopy programme at the Diabetes Malaysia Sarawak Branch headquarters here on Saturday.
Dr Sim said the prevalence of diabetes in Sarawak is between 17 and 18 per cent, compared with about 21 per cent nationwide.
The Minister of Public Health, Housing and Local Government said overnutrition is partly rooted in the mindset developed during times of food scarcity, when people were taught to finish everything on their plates.
“In recent years, there is plenty of food. When we were young, we didn’t have so much food, and we developed the habit of always finishing everything on the plate because we felt it was a waste not to. You may love the food, but nobody loves you for overeating.
“What I mean is, as long as you eat enough, that is sufficient. Don’t overeat,” he said.
The fundoscopy programme was organised by the Diabetes Malaysia Sarawak Branch in collaboration with the Sarawak General Hospital (SGH).
Dr Sim said fundoscopy, an eye examination used to detect eye diseases as well as systemic conditions such as diabetes, is important in raising public awareness of the need for early detection.
He added that Sarawak General Hospital continues to record diabetes-related amputations, lamenting that some patients are aware of their condition but fail to take it seriously or seek early treatment.
Emphasising that prevention is better than cure, he urged Sarawakians to undergo regular health check-ups and to “love yourself”.
Also present were Diabetes Malaysia Sarawak Branch chairman Dr William Voon, SGH Head of the Eye Department Dr Adeline Kueh, and others. — The Borneo Post

Title: Roofs torn off as fierce winds lash Sibu Jaya, no injuries reported
Article:
SIBU, July 18 —Several houses in Sibu Jaya sustained roof damage after strong winds accompanied by a heavy downpour swept through the area at about 3pm today.
Most of the affected homeowners were away when the storm struck, leaving neighbours to witness roofs being ripped off by the powerful winds.
No injuries were reported.
A resident, who declined to be named, said the weather deteriorated rapidly before strong winds battered the neighbourhood.
“It happened very quickly. We heard a loud noise and saw roof sheets being blown away.
“Some of the affected homeowners were still at work, so neighbours contacted them after the storm,” the resident said.
Another resident, who also requested anonymity, said neighbours immediately checked on the damaged houses and worked together to minimise further losses.
“Everyone came out after the rain eased to inspect the damage. We helped move belongings away from the rain and covered exposed parts of the houses as best we could while waiting for the owners to return,” the resident said.
Damages to the roof of a house in Sibu Jaya after strong winds on Saturday. — The Borneo Post pic
Resident Juntan Andy, 46, said the storm lasted only a short while but left extensive damage to several homes.
“The wind was unusually strong. Fortunately, no one was injured because many of the affected homeowners were not at home during the incident.
“We are thankful neighbours were around to keep an eye on the houses,” he said.
A car porch and awning severely damaged after being torn apart by strong winds during a heavy downpour in Sibu Jaya on Saturday. — The Borneo Post pic
Residents are hoping the relevant authorities will conduct damage assessments and provide assistance to affected families.
The number of houses affected and the extent of the losses have yet to be determined. — The Borneo Post

Title: Muhyiddin claims Bersatu is Parliament’s only true Opposition amid shifting alliances
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 18 — Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin has claimed Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia is now the country’s only “standalone” Opposition party, arguing that it must remain independent as the country’s sole credible check on the government amid shifting political alliances.
In a Facebook post today, the Bersatu president said the party had returned to the role it played when it was founded in the wake of the 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB) scandal, but now faced an even tougher political landscape.
“Today, Bersatu stands as an Opposition party, just as it did at the beginning of its journey.
“In fact, the challenges are even greater now because Bersatu is the only standalone Opposition party in the Malaysian Parliament,” he wrote.
Muhyiddin argued that Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Barisan Nasional (BN) were both part of the federal government, while Perikatan Nasional’s (PN) political cooperation with BN had, in his view, effectively turned the coalition into a supporter of the government.
The comments come as strains deepen within the Opposition following PAS’ decision to cooperate with BN in the Negeri Sembilan state election, exposing divisions over PN’s future direction.
Muhyiddin said Bersatu had no choice but to endure the political pressure because “there is now a major void in Malaysian politics”, claiming no other party was capable of providing effective checks and balances or representing Malaysians struggling with the rising cost of living.
He also accused BN and PH of sharing collective responsibility for policies he said had burdened the public, citing higher diesel and petrol prices, persistent cost-of-living pressures and what he described as the government’s failure to deliver institutional reforms.
Turning his criticism towards BN and Umno, Muhyiddin accused them of shifting blame while benefiting from being in government before appealing to Malay-Muslim unity for political gain.
“Believe me, this is nothing more than political theatre designed to deceive the Malays and the wider public in the pursuit of power.”
He said Bersatu would remain steadfast in its founding mission to defend the people’s interests and advance the country despite mounting political challenges.

Title: Three Form One boys in Kangar under police investigation over alleged bullying that left classmate with fractured ankle
Article:
KANGAR, July 18 — Three Form One students at a secondary school here have been remanded for two days from today to assist investigations into the alleged bullying of a schoolmate that left him with a fractured left ankle.
Kangar police chief ACP Yusharifuddin Mohd Yusop said police received a report on the incident at 11.27 pm on Thursday.
He said the victim’s 39-year-old mother had earlier been informed by his class teacher at 12.28 pm that her son, also a Form One student, had slipped while entering the classroom and was unable to walk.
The mother picked up her son at about 1 pm and took him to Tuanku Fauziah Hospital here, where a doctor confirmed that he had fractured his left ankle.
Yusharifuddin said that while at the hospital at about 7.28 pm, the boy told his mother that he had allegedly been bullied for several months, culminating in an incident that day which caused the injury.
The three students are believed to have kicked and hit the victim, thrown two large rubbish bins at his body and head, and spat on him.
They are also suspected of extorting the victim by demanding his pocket money.
The case is being investigated under Sections 324 and 507B of the Penal Code. — Bernama

Title: New Seremban orthopaedic centre launching this year aims to cut treatment costs by up to 50 per cent
Article:
SEREMBAN, July 18 — Bank Rakyat has provided financing totalling RM1.42 million to Limbcraft Innovation Sdn Bhd to develop an affordable orthopaedic centre here that is expected to offer treatment and surgery costs up to 50 per cent lower, depending on the type of procedure.
Entrepreneur and Cooperative Development Minister Steven Sim said the financing was channelled for the renovation of the premises as well as the purchase of machinery and equipment for Axis Orthopaedic Specialist Centre & Ambulatory Care Centre, which is expected to be launched this year.
He said the centre would be equipped with an operation theatre, day treatment wards and robotic rehabilitation facilities, while the financing was provided at a low profit rate of four per cent per annum.
“The benefits of financing should not stop at the growth of an enterprise, but rather should be translated into broader benefits for the people. In this case, ... it enables more people to get quality orthopaedic treatment at a more affordable cost,” he said in a statement today.
Earlier, Sim handed over a replica of the financing cheque to Limbcraft Innovation founder, who is also an orthopaedic surgeon, Dr Eswaran Nadarajan.
Meanwhile, Dr Eswaran said the establishment of the medical centre stemmed from the experience of seeing patients who had to wait for weeks, even months, to get treatment at government hospitals or pay between RM20,000 and RM30,000 for treatment at private hospitals. — Bernama

Title: Busker’s bid for Sikamat ends before it begins after election paperwork snag
Article:
SEREMBAN, July 18 — Government retiree Bujang Abu’s aspiration to contest as an independent candidate for the Sikamat state seat in the 16th Negeri Sembilan State Election fell through after documents required by the Election Commission (EC) were found to be incomplete.
Bujang, 75, had to withdraw at the eleventh hour after being informed that documents related to his expenditure from the previous election had not been submitted to the EC.
He acknowledged the matter stemmed from his own failure to complete the required documentation before the nomination process.
“During the nomination process, I was informed that I had not submitted the expenditure statement for the 2023 state election. I was supposed to submit the statement to the EC after the election.
